headline: Transcript: Reeves’ £30bn treasure hunt
summary: George Parker talks to Sam Fleming, Stephen Bush and Robert Shrimsley
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Political Fix podcast episode: ‘Reeves’ £30bn treasure hunt’

[AI SHIFT NEWSLETTER ADVERTISEMENT]

[MUSIC PLAYING]

George ParkerWelcome to Political Fix from the Financial Times with me, George Parker. So the hunt is on for what economists reckon will be at least £30bn to fill the fiscal gap in time for Budget day. And the prime minister’s deliberate evasion when challenged over a central tenet of Labour’s manifesto pledge offered a clue about where the money might be found.

Kemi Badenoch voice clipLast year, Labour promised not to increase income tax, not to increase national insurance and not to increase VAT. Does the prime minister still stand by his promises?

Keir Starmer voice clipThe Budget is on the 26th of November and we will lay out our plans, but I can tell the House now that we will build a stronger economy, we will cut NHS waiting lists and deliver a better future for our country.

Kemi Badenoch voice clipWell, well, well, Mr Speaker.
